title: Agentrys a New Paradigm for Semiconductor Engineering at 2026 DAC - Semiwiki
gist: EDA startup Agentrys is pioneering what it calls Agentic Design Automation (ADA) — proprietary, customer-owned AI agents that run behind a company's firewall on its existing EDA infrastructure and learn from its own design data — starting with functional verification (stimulus, checker, and assertion generation) and expanding into physical implementation.

themes.theme: Agentic Design Automation as the next EDA paradigm

insights.claim: Agentrys argues the next transformation beyond today's frontier AI (which generates RTL, scripts, assertions, testbenches, and assists with debug) is to automate and continuously improve the engineering workflows themselves, customized to each design team's own methodology — what it calls Agentic Design Automation (ADA).
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Distinguishes 'automating individual tasks' (current AI-in-EDA state) from 'automating and improving the workflow itself' as the next value layer — a framework for identifying which EDA AI vendors are targeting deeper, more defensible integration versus point features.
insights.quote: We believe the next transformation is to automate and continuously improve the engineering workflows that determine product quality, engineering productivity, and time-to-market.

insights.claim: Agentrys's core competitive argument is that AI advantage must come from something a customer's team owns — its own methodology and data — so its agents run behind the customer's firewall, learn from that company's own designs, and compound as a proprietary asset rather than being a rentable, commoditized capability.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Articulates a data-moat business model thesis (customer-owned, compounding agents vs. shared/rented AI capability) directly relevant to how EDA AI vendors will differentiate and retain customers as agentic tools proliferate.
insights.quote: So, the agents should be yours. They should learn from your designs, run behind your firewall, and get better with every project. That is the difference between renting capability everyone else can rent and building an asset that compounds for you alone.

insights.claim: Agentrys's initial product focus is functional verification — testbench stimulus generation, checker generation, and assertion creation — with active customer engagements already underway in physical implementation and optimization, and a roadmap extending to RTL design, analog design, and system design.
insights.speaker: Daniel Nenni
insights.so_what: Verification-first, physical-implementation-second is the specific sequencing of Agentrys's product rollout — a concrete milestone map for tracking whether the company executes on its stated roadmap into RTL and analog design.
insights.quote: Our initial focus is functional verification — testbench stimulus generation, checker generation, and assertion creation. Next, we are extending into physical implementation and optimization, where we are already in active customer engagements.
